context: Tests whether the agent chooses appropriate text-processing tools (awk for structured log parsing with calculations, grep/sort/uniq for filtering), avoids useless cat and ls|grep anti-patterns, uses a single awk pass where multiple metrics can be gathered at once, and correctly quotes variables including file path arguments.

checklist.name: awk for structured parsing
checklist.description: Script uses awk (not grep or sed alone) to parse the structured log fields (status code, URL, IP) from the access log

checklist.name: Single-pass awk for multiple stats
checklist.description: Status code counts (2xx, 3xx, 4xx, 5xx) are computed in a single awk invocation rather than separate passes over the file

checklist.name: No useless cat
checklist.description: Script does NOT use `cat file | awk` or `cat file | grep` patterns — passes the file directly as an argument to awk/grep/sort

checklist.name: No ls pipe to grep
checklist.description: Script does NOT use `ls | grep` to filter files

checklist.name: Quoted file path variable
checklist.description: The log file path variable is quoted when used in commands (e.g. awk '...' "${log_file}") to handle paths with spaces

checklist.name: validate_file or equivalent
checklist.description: Script checks that the input file exists and is readable before attempting to process it

checklist.name: Correct output produced
checklist.description: Running the script against the provided inputs/access.log produces output that includes a total request count and at least one status class breakdown

checklist.name: Usage function present
checklist.description: Script defines a usage() or help() function invoked when the file argument is missing
